title: 'Uninformed, in poor taste': India slams Donald Trump over 'hell hole' remark
content: India has labeled US President Donald Trump's "hell hole" remark on the country as "uninformed" and "inappropriate." The MEA spokesperson stated the comments do not reflect the reality of the strong India-US relationship, which is built on mutual respect and shared interests. Trump had reshared a post on Truth Social using derogatory terms for India and China.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 m
content: Michael and Susan Dell have pledged $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This significant donation will focus on integrating AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education. The project, expected to open by 2030, aims to enhance Austin's healthcare capabilities and advance medical innovation.

title: Trump's 'Gold Card' visa: One cleared, hundreds await as $1 million residency plan rolls out
content: The Trump administration's 'Gold Card' visa program, offering US residency for a $1 million fee, has approved only one applicant to date. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick stated hundreds more are under review, with all applicants undergoing extensive vetting and paying a $15,000 processing fee. The initiative aims to attract wealthy and skilled individuals to the US.

title: Why are CSK players wearing black armbands vs MI in IPL 2026 match?
content: CSK players were wearing black armbands due to CSK pacer Mukesh Choudhary’s mother passing away on Tuesday after a year-long battle with illness.

title: Iran’s IRGC warns it may cut undersea internet cables in Persian Gulf
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard has warned that undersea data cables in the Strait of Hormuz are highly vulnerable, potentially causing severe internet outages across the Persian Gulf. These vital digital arteries carry a significant portion of regional internet traffic and support major AI infrastructure, impacting global communication, finance, and cloud services.

title: Bhooth Bangla BO: Akshay Kumar starrer marches towards Rs 100 crore in week 1
content: 'Bhooth Bangla', directed by Priyadarshan and starring Akshay Kumar, Tabu, Paresh Rawal, Wamiqa Gabbi, Rajpal Yadav, Mithila Palkar and Asrani, is closing in on the Rs. 100 crore India net milestone as it completes its first week in theatres. With a cumulative India net of Rs. 79.02 crore and a worldwide gross of Rs. 127.37 crore after seven days, the U/A certified horror-comedy has delivered a strong opening week despite competing against 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' in its fifth week and a full IPL 2026 season. Marking Akshay Kumar and Priyadarshan's reunion after 16 years, the 145-minute supernatural thriller follows Arjun Acharya confronting the demonic entity Vadhusur in his ancestral home in Mangalpur, featuring a nostalgic ensemble of Paresh Rawal, Rajpal Yadav and the late Asrani.

title: 'We are installing tracking software in your machines': Meta to US employees
content: Meta is installing new tracking software on US employees' computers to capture mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes for AI training. This initiative aims to improve AI agents' ability to perform work tasks autonomously, like using shortcuts. Meta assures employees the data won't be used for performance reviews, only for enhancing AI models.

title: Sanju Samson scripts history vs MI in IPL, becomes only CSK batter to...
content: Sanju Samson completed his century on the last ball of the innings with a four off Krish Bhagat's bowl. That was his second century of the season, his second for CSK and his fifth in the IPL history.

title: 'Naughty or nice?' Trump ranks Nato allies over support in Iran war
content: The White House has reportedly created a 'naughty and nice' list for Nato allies, ranking them on contributions and support during the Iran war. This initiative aims to pressure nations that did not align with US positions, potentially impacting future military cooperation and strategic support.

title: NFL trade rumor: Commanders eye $120M 49ers star for Daniels boost
content: The Washington Commanders recorded a brutal run on the field in the last season as the team managed to win just 5 games while losing in 12 in 2025. This was a major decline since the 2024 season, when the Washington Commanders recorded 12 wins and 5 losses. With Jayden Daniels sustaining major injuries, it seems the team is focused on fixing its roster ahead of the new season.

title: 2026 NFL mock draft: Picks for Falcons, Panthers, Lions, Packers
content: The 2026 NFL Draft is finally here. The analysts have finalized predictions for college prospects. The Atlanta Falcons are linked to three quarterbacks, while the Carolina Panthers are tied to Oregon Ducks safety and tight end. Several experts also highlight potential picks for the Detroit Lions and Green Bay Packers, including offensive linemen and defensive tackles.

title: Dana White: UFC to break strict rule for Trump White House fig
content: UFC Freedom 250 will take place on June 14, 2026, at the White House as part of US 250th independence celebrations and President Donald Trump’s 80th birthday. The seven-fight card features major title bouts, but the key change is UFC CEO Dana White breaking tradition by including a live national anthem performance for the first time in years.

title: The optics deficit: Will missing Stalin-Rahul ‘brotherhood’ hurt alliance in Tamil Nadu?
content: The absence of joint rallies between Rahul Gandhi and alliance partners like MK Stalin in Tamil Nadu raises concerns about Congress's coalition strategy. This visual gap fuels speculation and opposition attacks, contrasting with proactive alliance building seen from other INDIA bloc leaders like Arvind Kejriwal.

title: 'Modi ji what are you scared of?' Kharge targets PM over Trump's 'hellhole' remark
content: Congress President Mallikarjun Kharge criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi for his silence on Donald Trump's derogatory remarks about India. Kharge questioned Modi's stance, highlighting India's contributions to the US and alleging that the government has failed to protect national interests in past dealings with the US.

title: 'Trump has said India a great country': US in firefight mode after president's 'hellhole' remark
content: US President Donald Trump lauded India as a "great country" with a "very good friend at the top," following a controversial social media repost that disparaged India and China. The US Embassy clarified Trump's positive remarks, while India's MEA acknowledged the reports. The repost criticized immigration, alleging bias and a lack of loyalty from certain immigrant groups.

title: Big blow for Spain: Injured Yamal ruled out for season ahead of FIFA World Cup
content: "The player will undergo conservative treatment. Lamine Yamal will miss the rest of the season and is expected to be available for the World Cup," which runs from June 11-July 19 this year.

title: Mumtaz on Feroz Khan: ‘Our bond was platonic,’ recalls his charm
content: Veteran actress Mumtaz recently clarified her close bond with the late Feroz Khan, emphasizing their friendship despite his undeniable charm. She revealed Feroz had a girlfriend during their film collaborations, including 'Apraadh' where she did her only bikini scene. Their children, Natasha and Fardeen, later had an arranged marriage, continuing their families' enduring connection.

title: Shastri almost blunders at toss; forgets Ruturaj Gaikwad's name - Watch
content: While conducting the toss, Shastri introduced Mumbai Indians skipper Hardik Pandya but stopped briefly before naming Gaikwad.

title: IRS officer’s daughter murder: Autopsy shows 'multiple abrasions, signs of struggle'
content: Autopsy findings in the rape and murder of an IRS officer's daughter reveal multiple injuries indicating a violent struggle. The 22-year-old victim sustained abrasions, contusions, and a nasal bone fracture. The accused, a domestic help, allegedly used a spare key to enter the apartment and assaulted her before fleeing with valuables.

title: No Dhoni again; CSK captain says 'thanks to legend' at the toss
content: MS Dhoni's highly anticipated return to IPL 2026 action was not to be, as he remained sidelined due to a calf strain. While Dhoni was seen engaging with a robotic dog during practice, fans continue to await his comeback. Mumbai Indians won the toss and chose to field against Chennai Super Kings.

title: Late nights, long screens: Why sleep is quietly slipping away
content: Young people in urban India are experiencing a growing pattern of disrupted sleep, with late nights and insufficient rest becoming routine. This consistent loss of sleep is gradually impacting their daily functioning, leading to reduced energy, focus, and overall performance. The article highlights the subtle yet significant consequences of this widespread sleep deficit.

title: 'Deadlock over': EU unlocks $105bn loan for Ukraine, slaps new sanctions on Russia
content: In a landmark decision, the European Union has greenlit an impressive €90 billion loan to Ukraine alongside a new wave of sanctions targeting Russia, effectively breaking a prolonged deadlock. This vital financial support, designated for military and fiscal stability, follows Hungary and Slovakia's recent agreement to resume oil flows through a major pipeline.

title: Sara Arjun to play Madhubala; actresses who recreated her iconic look
content: The Madhubala biopic is finally moving forward with Sara Arjun set to portray the legendary actress. Sanjay Leela Bhansali is producing the film, slated for a July 2026 shoot.  This project highlights Madhubala's enduring legacy, with actresses like Sridevi, Madhuri Dixit, Jacqueline Fernandez, and Kangana Ranaut having previously paid tribute to her iconic style and grace.

title: Fight in PSL: Faheem points bat at Amir after wicket, tension erupts - Watch
content: The moment came on the third ball of the 17th over of the first innings when Amir bowled a slow bouncer to Faheem Ashraf. Faheem tried to play an upper-cut but edged it to wicketkeeper Mohammad Rizwan.

title: 'Will you just khi khi?' Oppn calls PM 'weak' over silence on Trump's 'hellhole' post
content: A political storm has erupted after US President Donald Trump reshared a post labeling India a "hell hole." Opposition parties, including the Congress and Mahua Moitra, have sharply criticized the remarks and Prime Minister Narendra Modi's perceived silence, questioning his leadership. The controversy stems from a podcast by Michael Savage, which Trump amplified on his platform.

title: Kalki on post-divorce bond with Anurag Kashyap, no grudges
content: Kalki Koechlin shared insights on navigating post-divorce dynamics with ex-husband Anurag Kashyap, emphasizing therapy's long-term benefits and the necessity of initial non-communication. She advocates for creating distance, suggesting a period of separation equal to the relationship's length, and processing emotions with trusted individuals before considering reconnection.

title: 'If didi had given money ...': Man who raped, killed IRS officer's daughter shows no remorse
content: A former domestic help, Rahul Meena, allegedly used a spare key to enter a south Delhi apartment and rape and murder his former employer's daughter. He is suspected of strangling her, attempting to access a locker, and fleeing after stealing cash and jewelry. Meena, described as remorseful, is also being investigated for a similar crime in Rajasthan.

title: Watch: Stones thrown at Bengal BJP Agnimitra Paul's car, rear window shattered
content: BJP leader Agnimitra Paul's car was vandalized in a stone-pelting incident in Asansol after she exited a polling booth. Paul alleged a deliberate attempt to influence the elections and filed a complaint. Contesting from Asansol South, she expressed confidence in the BJP's prospects, calling the election a 'war to save Bengal' focused on jobs and development.

title: Mexico safety concerns rise after deadly pyramid attack, president responds
content: A deadly shooting at Teotihuacan has intensified scrutiny of Mexico’s security readiness ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The planned attack, which killed a tourist and injured several others, exposed gaps at a major landmark. While authorities call it isolated, the incident underscores broader concerns about safeguarding visitors during a globally significant tournament approaching quickly.

title: Mike Vrabel–Dianna Russini drama worsens amid new affair claims
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and the popular NFL insider who used to work at The New York Times’ Dianna Russini, continue to be in the eye of the storm as their alleged affair rumors swirl as their spouses remain distant. Though both of them have denied engaging in an affair and betraying their respective spouses, it seems the story has taken a turn for the worse.

title: Indian Seafarer Among Crew Of Ship Seized By Iran, Centre Says He's Safe
content: Two of the three foreign-flagged commercial vessels fired upon by Iranian forces in the strait on Wednesday had 22 Indian seafarers.

title: Russian Town Sees Toxic Rain After Ukraine Strike, Officials Issue Warning
content: Kyiv struck oil facilities in Tuapse on Monday, triggering a huge blaze and sending plumes of thick black smoke into the sky over the city.

title: West Bengal Elections 1st Phase Voting LIVE Updates | Bengal Sees Highest-Ever Voter Turnout At 92.59%
content: West Bengal Assembly Elections 2026 Phase 1 Voting LIVE Updates: Around 3.6 crore voters in 152 of the state's 294 constituencies are expected to vote today.

title: West Bengal, Tamil Nadu Elections 2026 LIVE Updates: Highest-Ever Voter Turnout In West Bengal, Tamil Nadu
content: West Bengal, Tamil Nadu Election Voting 2026 LIVE Updates: Around 3.6 crore voters in 152 of Bengal's 294 constituencies across 16 districts are expected to vote in the first phase today.

title: "Bengal Chief Minister Will Be From BJP": Amit Shah To NDTV At Rally
content: West Bengal assembly election 2026: "We will definitely win this time. We are fighting in seats seen to be difficult to win," Amit Shah said

title: BJP Leads Big Protest Against Opposition in Guwahati Over Women's Quota Bill
content: The demonstration, which saw participation from women across different sections of society, was organised to condemn Opposition parties accused of blocking the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill

title: Warner Bros Shareholders Approve Paramount's $81 Billion Takeover
content: Per a preliminary vote count on Thursday, the overwhelming majority of Warner Bros. Discovery shareholders voted in support of selling the entire business to Paramount for $31 a share.

title: On Camera, 'Red Liquid' Thrown On Iran's Exiled Prince Reza Pahlavi In Berlin
content: In the attack which was captured on camera, Iran's exiled Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi was walking on a pavement with his security team when a man walking behind him carrying a backpack threw a red liquid on him, believed to be tomato juice.

title: 600 km, 5 Hours: Meerut-Prayagraj Ganga Expressway To Be Inaugurated This Month
content: The Ganga Expressway is the country's only expressway that has trauma centres, which will provide swift treatment to accident victims.

title: "Made A Mistake": Ex-Help In Court On Killing IRS Officer's Daughter
content: Piecing together the chilling details of the Wednesday morning murder, police officials said Rahul Meena is a man without remorse.

title: Exclusive: Ex-Army Chief Denies Being "Abandoned" During China Face-Off
content: General Manoj Mukund Naravane (retired) told NDTV that the orders that he received were in fact unambiguous and that Indian soldiers had the right to open fire if their safety was at stake

title: Hackers Steal $2.5 Million From Sri Lanka Government, Highest In Its History
content: The money was destined as debt repayment to Australia, finance ministry secretary Harshana Suriyapperuma said.

title: Adani Energy Solutions Sees 32% Profit Growth In FY26
content: Driven by recent project wins, the company's aggregate transmission under construction pipeline stands at Rs 71,779 crore.

title: Polling Staff Suspended For Leaving Booth Unattended During Voting In Bengal's Pingla
content: The entire polling team at booth No. 9 stepped out together around noon, reportedly for lunch, leaving the booth unmanned for some time, the official said.

title: 8 Killed, 2 Injured As Vehicle Falls Into Gorge In Uttarakhand's Chamba
content: The victims, residents of Thyoldhar village in the Ghansali, were returning home after performing the last rites of a villager in Haridwar.

title: Trump Orders US Navy To "Shoot And Kill" Boats Putting Mines In Hormuz
content: Donald Trump's post on social media came shortly after the US military seized another tanker associated with the smuggling of Iranian oil.

title: "We're Watching": On Poll Day, Mahua Moitra Reveals Locations Of 2 BJP Leaders
content: The 51-year-old Trinamool MP questioned the presence of Swadesh Singh, a professor at Delhi's Satyavati College, and Saurabh Singh, a former BJP MLA from Chhattisgarh, in Bengal on the day of voting.

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: Trump Orders US Navy "To Shoot And Kill Any Boat" Putting Mines In Hormuz
content: US-Israel-Iran War Ceasefire News Live Updates: The standoff between the US and Iran has effectively choked off nearly all exports through the strait -- where 20 per cent of the world's traded oil passes in peacetime -- with no end in sight.

title: Trump shares right-wing anchor’s rant against Indians, MEA terms it ‘inappropriate’
content: The MEA response came after Opposition members and former diplomats criticised the government for not offering a stronger response to the U.S.

title: NHRC issues notices to Centre, States over poor adoption of NCERT, SCERT textbooks in private schools
content: A complaint received by the NHRC had alleged that substitution of textbooks published by the NCERT or the SCERT with private materials translates to heavy financial burden on families and violates NEP norms

title: Bombay HC pulls up NIA for delays in 2006 Malegaon blast investigation
content: The Division Bench observed that the Special Judge under the National Investigation Agency (NIA) Act had not applied judicial mind to the materials placed before the court and had considered evidence that is not admissible in law to frame charges

title: Nepal’s customs duty is the outcome of a ‘pre-existing provision’, will not affect ‘household goods’: India
content: The rule makes it mandatory for travellers carrying goods worth more than one hundred Nepali rupee (NPR) to pay customs duty on the goods. It is aimed at protecting traders in border towns who complain that visitors do not buy things from them as they enter the country after buying things from the Indian side of the border

title: Analysis: Why there is a ‘Pawar play’ over political legacy in Baramati
content: Aspirations of the younger generation of the Pawar family, the BJP’s efforts to make in-roads, and the dilemma of the NCP and NCP (SP) cadre raise questions about the future of the Pawar bastion

title: Nirmala Sitharaman urges bankers to brace for AI threats amid concerns over Anthropic's Mythos
content: During the meeting, the finance minister urged the Indian Banks’ Association (IBA) to develop a coordinated institutional mechanism to respond swiftly and effectively to any such threats

title: Naidu directs officials to develop Amaravati into revenue-generating growth hub
content: Chairing the 60th meeting of Andhra Pradesh Capital Region Development Authority, the Chief Minister emphasises that the capital’s infrastructure should reflect Telugu culture while featuring iconic, high-rise structures

title: Book release controversy: SSUS to initiate inquiry
content: The release of Sri Tirthapada Puranam, a biography of Chattampi Swamikal published by the varsity, was reportedly cancelled at the last minute after it was found that only four volumes had been printed, while the other bound volumes kept in a box were blank

title: Relief as West Bengal sees bonhomie amid bitter political rivalry
content: Trinamool and BJP candidates at Kharagpur Sadar shake hands, while BJP candidate in Taldangra constituency touches the feet of his rival Trinamool nominee

title: Watch: Above 92% turnout in Bengal, 85% in Tamil Nadu amid poll violence | Above the Fold | 23.04.2026
content: In today’s episode, we look at the key election trends in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal, tracking major shifts ahead of polling. We also cover the political row in the United States over birthright citizenship, rising tensions in West Asia around the Strait of Hormuz, and a significant change in West Bengal’s campaign landscape, and more

title: West Bengal Elections 2026 highlights: First phase of polling complete; estimated voter turnout crosses 92%
content: Phase 1 polling which saw 'highest ever' turnout saw isolated incidents of violence in several pockets

title: West Bengal polls: Modi and Mamata spar over jhaal muri
content: After voting for the first phase of Assembly polls in West Bengal, the campaign has picked up for the final phase on April 29

title: Tamil Nadu elections 2026 highlights: Over 85% voter turnout in State for the first time since Independence
content: Limited bus services affect polling day travel; robots dressed in 'dhavanis' (half-sarees) welcome and assist voters in several booths across the State; several young voters show up in colour-coded outfits, purportedly in support of TVK

title: Tamil Nadu elections 2026: Over 84% voter turnout in the State, highest in its electoral history
content: Chief Election Commissioner Gyanesh Kumar said that it was the highest percentage of polling in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu. “[The] ECI salutes each voter of West Bengal and Tamil Nadu,” he said

title: West Bengal election 2026: Record turnout of over 92% in first phase amidst isolated incidents of violence
content: Before the 2026 elections, the highest turnout was recorded in 2011 when 84.33% voters exercised their franchise; the elections in 2011 saw Trinamool Congress defeating the 34-year Left Front government

title: Kerala on the brink of heatwave, Chief Minister calls for ‘self-lockdown’
content: IMD Director says though heatwave has not been technically declared, the hot and humid conditions are at times comparable to heatwave. Intense heat, coupled with high humidity, is likely to persist at least till April 27

title: Congress Govt on back foot as Telangana RTC strike paralyses services, exposes gaps in crisis management
content: The situation escalated sharply with a series of distressing incidents involving RTC staff, intensifying public anger and political pressure

title: Supreme Court stays Chhattisgarh HC verdict sentencing Amit Jogi for life in 2003 murder
content: A Bench of Justices Vikram Nath, Sandeep Mehta and Vijay Bishnoi issued notice to the CBI and stayed the operation of the verdict on the “facts and circumstances of the case”

title: 59% voter turnout recorded in bypoll to Gujarat’s Umreth Assembly seat
content: The contest is primarily between BJP candidate Harshad Govindbhai Parmar and Congress nominee Bhrugurajsinh Chauhan

title: Iranian student has right to use swimming pool: rights panel
content: The petitioner who is married to an Indian had complained that a swimming pool assistant racially and verbally abused her and some friends claiming they were foreign citizens and not allowed to the use the pool at Greenfield Stadium in Thiruvananthapuram

title: Telangana’s maximum temperatures again surge to near 45°C
content: Thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds forecast in 17 Telangana districts on Thursday

title: Vice-President urged not to share dais with Central University V-C during convocation today
content: Aland MLA says he will boycott Wednesday’s convocation being organised by the university in Kalaburagi

title: Lok Ayukta orders probe against police, MVD, Cars24 for selling vehicle without transferring ownership
content: The seller found himself named as a respondent in a motor accident claim case following an accident involving the car, which was no longer in his possession. He had also received tickets for traffic violations committed using the vehicle

title: Olympic medallist Sakshi Malik opens SRM University’s sports festival
content: Wrestler highlights discipline and fitness as varsity unveils plans for state-of-the-art sports complex and honours student achievers

title: Congress attacks government for permitting ethanol in aircraft fuel
content: Party’s Kerala unit says the blended fuel should be first used in PM Narendra Modi’s aircraft or Union Minister Nitin Gadkari’s private jet for a couple of years, prove it is safe, and only then consider introducing it to the public

title: K.C. Venugopal brings out book on his speeches
content: The book Nerinoppam: Porattangal Nilapadukal is a collection of select speeches made by him in the Kerala Assembly, Lok Sabha, and Rajya Sabha over the past three decades

title: MA&UD reviews implementation of new solid waste management rules
content: Principal Secretary notes 16.57% revenue growth in 2025-26; ULBs tasked with ensuring 100% door-to-door waste collection and source segregation

title: Winning trust vote by NDA is a foregone conclusion: JD(U)
content: A one-day special session of Bihar Assembly has been called to prove majority of new government led by BJP’s first Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary

title: Eight killed after vehicle plunges into gorge in Uttarakhand
content: The victims were returning home after attending a cremation when the accident occurred near the Chamba–Koti stretch in Tehri Garhwal district

title: Manipur rights group opposes powers to lower-ranked police officers under UAPA
content: An April 22 notification to implement Section 43A of the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act allows a wider pool of officers to arrest, search, and seize across the State with immediate effect

title: Andhra Pradesh to unify skill departments under single commissionerate
content: Secretary says ‘One District – One Skill’ vision will align training with local economic potential; APSSDC targets 20 lakh jobs through stronger placement ecosystem

title: India, Egypt advance their defence cooperation at Cairo meet
content: Both sides agreed on a bilateral defence cooperation plan for 2026–27, focusing on expanding structured military engagements, intensifying joint training exchanges and boosting maritime security cooperation

title: Quiet Tarluvada readies for Google AI data centre
content: Nestled in Anandapuram mandal outside Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation limits, the hamlet will host Google’s first hyperscale 1 GW AI data centre outside the United States; the foundation stone is to be laid on April 28.

title: Directives issued to reduce number of snakebite incidents
content: An awareness campaign will be conducted at the ward-level across the State in association with local self-government bodies, focusing on the precautions to be taken during the summer and the breeding season of snakes

title: Karnataka’s unique issue with alcohol
content: Fewer people drink in Karnataka, but those who do, drink more; per-capita alcohol consumption in the State is about 9.1 litres per person per year, compared with the national average of 6.4 litres

title: Broader Left unity is the way back into the West Bengal Assembly: Dipankar Bhattacharya
content: The Left’s vote share has declined drastically in West Bengal and it needs a ‘critical mass’ to get back into the Assembly, says the Communist Party of India (Marxist-Leninist) Liberation general secretary; he calls the SIR exercise ‘the beginning of disempowerment’, says the Communist movement must pay attention to rural Bengal and address the issues of the new generation, such as the Noida protests

title: BJP government scored ‘double F’ on fuel, fertiliser security, says Kharge
content: Mallikarjun Kharge alleged that the government was attempting to divert attention from its “failures” and “serious Epstein file allegations” through delimitation

title: TTD miffed at two contract priests of Vontimitta temple meeting Jagan in Pulivendula
content: Deputy Executive Officer serves notice on them seeking their explanation; nothing wrong in the duo meeting their elected representative, argue YSRCP MPs

title: KLE Society PU students do well
content:
Students of pre-university colleges run by KLE Society have done well in II PU examinations, KLE Society chairman Amit Kore told reporters in Belagavi on Wednesday.

He was speaking after felicitating students on KLE RLS campus.
